text: "Of course, most fences aren't poisoned on top!"
speaker: bonehead

text: "Baba Yaga's hut will squat down if you say the rhyme."
speaker: bonehead

text: "The rhyme is:  'Hut of brown, now sit down'."
speaker: bonehead

text: "My time is important, and I'm very busy, as you can well see.  Ask me about something important."
speaker: bonehead

text: You place the glowing gem inside the skull.
speaker: narrator

text: "I can see!  I have eyes again!!"
speaker: bonehead

text: "Yeccchhh!  Is THAT what you look like???"
speaker: bonehead

text: "Oh well!  Have fun visiting Baba Yaga.  And good luck....you'll need it!"
speaker: bonehead

text: "Baba Yaga does not welcome strangers!  You have to deal with me before you can enter."
speaker: bonehead

text: "Baba Yaga is one tough magic user.  If you're so stupid that you want to enter, perhaps we can make a deal."
speaker: bonehead

text: "So you're back!  Changed your mind about making a little deal, hero?"
speaker: bonehead

text: "Did you get it?  Did you get my glowing gem???"
speaker: bonehead

text: "So you made it back, did you?  She is expecting you."
speaker: bonehead

text: "You again??  Do you really want to go back in there?"
speaker: bonehead

text: "I hope you can remember the rhyme."
speaker: bonehead

text: You really brought down the house!  Too bad you were under it at the time.
speaker: narrator

text: There goes Baba Yaga.  Now you've REALLY made her soar!
speaker: narrator

text: From a distance, you hear the witch intone:
speaker: narrator

text: "Oops!  I very nearly forgot..."
speaker: baba-yaga

text:
"Hear me, oh Powers
Of Mana and More:
Turn this fool back
As he was before."
speaker: baba-yaga

text: As you contemplate the occurrences of the last few minutes, you conclude that you'd better find her mandrake root...and fast!
speaker: narrator

text: "What an awful creature, to turn me into a FROG!", you say to yourself.  "Someday, I must return the favor."
say: What an awful creature, to turn me into a FROG! Someday, I must return the favor.
speaker: hero

text: "I am sorry, but I'm much too broken to talk about such things right now.  I have lost everything!"
speaker: abdulla

text: The merchant consumes your meal in record time.
speaker: narrator

text: He then procedes to finish your drink in one gulp.
speaker: narrator

text: The merchant gratefully accepts your food ration, and consumes it in record time.
speaker: narrator

text: You can't give what you don't have.
speaker: narrator

text: The merchant finishes your drink in one gulp.
speaker: narrator

text: It is the merchant's hat.
speaker: narrator

text: It is the merchant's turban.
speaker: narrator

text: "There were about twelve brigands, including a Minotaur, if you can believe it!  Their leader was wearing a hooded cloak.  I could not see the face, but he had a high-pitched voice.  There was also some sort of warlock who giggled a lot."
speaker: abdulla

text: "Although worn with age, the brigand leader's cloak must have, at one time, been an exquisite garment.  No doubt it was stolen, as were all of my goods!"
speaker: abdulla

text: "Perhaps it was my eyes that deceived me, but I could swear there was Gnome blood in that odd magician."
speaker: abdulla

text: "The magician who travels with the brigands is no wizard."
speaker: abdulla

text: "It is my hat."
speaker: abdulla

text: "It is my turban."
speaker: abdulla

text: "I am Abdulla Doo, son of Ali, grandson of Hasan, and formerly Master Merchant of Shapeir.  Now I am but a penniless burden upon my friends."
speaker: abdulla

text: "Even though they, too, lost a fortune when the brigands stole from me, Shameen and Shema are caring for this frail shadow of a great man."
speaker: abdulla

text: "Oh, I cannot bear to talk about my family at this sad time!"
speaker: abdulla

text: "You must not have heard of the brigands who prey upon the innocent and unwary."
speaker: abdulla

text: "All that I once owned is gone, alas!"
speaker: abdulla

text: "Who would have thought that there could be such trouble entering this valley during the off-season?"
speaker: abdulla

text: "My good friends Shema and Shameen, owners of this inn, are the finest of all the Katta people that ever graced Shapeir!  But for their kindness, I would have starved long since."
speaker: abdulla

text: "If you have never seen such a beast before, you may count yourself lucky.  It is like a giant of a man with a bull's head."
speaker: abdulla

text: "Two weeks ago, I was about to become wealthy beyond my dreams.  I, Abdulla Doo, would be the first merchant into the valley this year.  But my life was shattered by those vile brigands!"
speaker: abdulla

text: "The band of brigands ambushed us just as we crossed the pass into the valley. They first used some magic which blinded us.  Then they overwhelmed my six guards and my assistants."
speaker: abdulla

text: "All of my trade goods were taken from me, right before my very eyes!  Why the brigand leader spared my life, I do not know.  I am now but a beggar, living off the generosity of my friends."
speaker: abdulla

text: "Ah, Shapeir!  Beautiful land of golden sands and shining sun! The heart of civilization!  Alas, she is plagued with fierce Djinni and Efreets, who seek to drive all men and Kattas from the land."
speaker: abdulla

text: "But I can speak no more of the homeland I shall never see again.  Instead, I will die in this cold, forsaken land, bereft of all I love!"
speaker: abdulla

text: "Alas, had I known magic, perhaps I could have turned the tide against those abominable brigands.  Their magic did not seem so strong, but they outnumbered my poor caravan by two to one!"
speaker: abdulla

text: "My guards are all gone, killed or run away like dogs!  I, too, would be lying dead had not the strange brigand leader spared my life, for what reason I know not!"
speaker: abdulla

text: "Your kindness overwhelms me.  I can tell you will someday be a great hero."
speaker: abdulla

text: "Now you will have a secret.  Among the items they stole from me was a magic rug.  I alone know the words to command it."
speaker: abdulla

text: "If you help recover my treasure, I will take you, Shameen, and Shema back to our land by way of the flying carpet.  Shapeir needs powerful heroes, too!"
speaker: abdulla

text: "Oh, it is indeed sad and dangerous times we live in when a man who struggles daily to keep from starving should be robbed of all his earthly possessions!"
speaker: abdulla

text: "Hello again, my friend."
speaker: abdulla

text: Looking at the Centaur's muscles, his hoe, and his four gleaming hooves, you get the feeling that hostility is not the best of ideas.
speaker: narrator

text: The farmer has painstakingly removed all the rocks from his fields.
speaker: narrator

text: Don't throw things here.  The farmer wouldn't appreciate it.
speaker: narrator

text: The majestic Centaur watches you carefully.
speaker: narrator

text: The handsome Centaur has a look of pride and dignity as he rakes his field.
speaker: narrator

text: There's a world of difference between a horse and a Centaur!
speaker: narrator

text: The Centaur is half man, half horse.
speaker: narrator

text: It is a sturdy iron rake with a wooden handle.
speaker: narrator

text: "You must go see my daughter, Hilde, who sells our fruits and vegetables on Market Street."
speaker: heinrich

text: Go ahead.  Ask him something.
speaker: narrator

text: "I am Heinrich Pferdefedern.  I live in town with my daughter Hilde, who sells our produce on Market Street."
speaker: heinrich

text: I'm sorry.  I know little about the Baron and his family.
speaker: heinrich

text: "Hilde is a bright little filly.  I'm very proud of her."
speaker: heinrich

text: "We Centaurs are a very ancient and proud people."
speaker: heinrich

text: "It is truly a sight to see."
speaker: heinrich

text: "There are a few carrots and potatoes left from last season, but I'm just starting to plant this year's crops."
speaker: heinrich

text: "I can grow enough on this little plot to feed my family and the village.  You should see this field at harvest time!"
speaker: heinrich

text: "The brigands attacked me a few months ago.  I am a strong fighter and my hooves are deadly, but there were too many of them."
speaker: heinrich

text: "They nearly killed me.  My right leg was broken, and there is no pride for a Centaur who cannot run."
speaker: heinrich

text: "If it had not been for the unlikely intervention of their leader, I would be dead."
speaker: heinrich

text: "The leader came up while the brigands attacked me and forced them to stop, ordering them not to hurt people from the town."
speaker: heinrich

text: "Then, to my amazement, the leader gave me a Healing Potion and had me carried to the Healer's house."
speaker: heinrich

text: "It was hard to judge the leader, whose face was obscured by a large helm, but there was compassion unlike a brigand."
speaker: heinrich

text: "The Healer lives just east of here.  She is very skilled with potions."
speaker: heinrich

text: "The late snow has made the mountains very pretty.  Fortunately, our valley is sheltered, so the frost has not come to my fields."
speaker: heinrich

text: "Fields must be carefully tended to produce the best crops."
speaker: heinrich

text: "Our stand is in the northeast corner of Spielburg.  The produce is not of the finest this time of year, but you will not find better than Pferdefedern fruit come harvest time."
speaker: heinrich

text: We grow fine fruit in our little orchard, and sell it at our stand in town.  There you will find fresh cherries in the spring, peaches in summer, and apples in the fall."
speaker: heinrich

text: Climbing this wall is too difficult for your level of skill.  Keep practicing.
speaker: narrator

text: You're not in a good spot for climbing the wall.
speaker: narrator

text: The town gate is wide open during the day.  You would look silly climbing the wall.
speaker: narrator

text: The field is hoed and fertilized.
speaker: narrator

text: The garden's dark dirt awaits a new day's sunshine.
speaker: narrator

text: You see a nicely manicured field.  A Centaur rakes the rows.
speaker: narrator

text: The trees have tiny fruit forming.
speaker: narrator

text: The forest surrounds the farmer's small fields and orchard.
speaker: narrator

text: The crops have yet to be planted.  There are only a few weeds and some grasses at edge of the forest.
speaker: narrator

text: You can see the roofs of Spielburg beyond the town wall.
speaker: narrator

text: You can see a small house.
speaker: narrator

text: Beyond the farmer's orchard, you see the forest.
speaker: narrator

text: Noticing that you are addressing him, the Centaur stops and gives you his attention.
speaker: narrator

text: The Centaur turns toward you.
speaker: narrator

text: The Centaur stops raking as you approach.
speaker: narrator

text: In a hole, hidden beneath the ancient stone, someone has placed a scroll.
speaker: narrator

text: The scroll is no longer here.
speaker: narrator

text: You see no scroll here.
speaker: narrator

text: The scroll vanishes even as you read the magical runes upon it.   You now have the knowledge to cast a "Calm" spell.
speaker: narrator

text: Your hunger has been totally satisfied.  You don't need another piece of fruit.
speaker: narrator

text: The stone has the words "Guruka's Peace" carved upon the top.  Funny.  You thought it said something else the last time you looked at it.
speaker: narrator

text: The stone has the words "Erana's Peace" carved upon the top.  Maybe you need glasses.
speaker: narrator

text: The stone has the words "Erana's Peace" carved upon the top.  There are some runes carved along the side.
speaker: narrator

text: It reads:  "If thy Will is Magic, so shall I share.  Open this Stone and claim what is there."
speaker: narrator

text: No amount of physical effort will be able to move this stone.
speaker: narrator

text: The meadow lies covered with a blanket of flowers, unusual for this early in the spring.  It is warm, even though surrounded by the late snows of winter.
speaker: narrator

text: The air has the fresh, clean scent of the mountains, accompanied by numerous perfume-like fragrances.
speaker: narrator

text: A large, carved stone lies flat on the ground.  You feel as though someone gentle was watching over you.  You feel that you are safe here.
speaker: narrator

text: All kinds of colorful and fragrant flowers and grasses cover the meadow.
speaker: narrator

text: The sky is clear.
speaker: narrator

text: The stars shine.
speaker: narrator

text: The sun feels warm.
speaker: narrator

text: The tree's blossoms are soft, green and fragrant.
speaker: narrator

text: The fruit glows and shimmers in the moonlight.  It produces a fragrance like a sun-ripened strawberry.
speaker: narrator

text: The fruit sparkles in the sunlight, yet it appears soft and fuzzy.
speaker: narrator

text: To the north, the high, snowy peaks of the Dragon Smoke mountains are clearly visible.
speaker: narrator

text: The meadow is an oasis at the edge of the snowline.  The rocky hillside looks very steep and slippery.
speaker: narrator

text: You are at the snowline.  All around you, except for the forest, the rocks and mountains glisten with slippery snow.
speaker: narrator

text: To the south, you see the deep forest through which you came to this meadow.
speaker: narrator

text: The stone has been moved.
speaker: narrator

text: The large stone appears to be ancient and deliberately placed.  Marks carved into the stone almost look like writing.
speaker: narrator

text: You see no holes.
speaker: narrator

text: The hole that was beneath the stone is empty.
speaker: narrator

text: Go over to the tree and pick some.
speaker: narrator

text: The sweet, juicy fruit of the tree is amazingly satisfying and refreshing.
speaker: narrator

text: The lovely blossoms should stay on the tree.
speaker: narrator

text: The fruit is very soft and juicy.   It would be impossible to keep in your pack.
speaker: narrator

text: The grasses in the meadow are covered over with flowers.
speaker: narrator

text: You take another handful of the lovely, fragrant flowers.
speaker: narrator

text: As you pick a variety of the sweet-smelling flowers, they seem to glow in your hands.  You put them safely away.
speaker: narrator

text: The smell reminds you of laughter.
speaker: narrator

text: There is no further purpose in casting the Open spell.
speaker: narrator

text: There is an aura of magic throughout this meadow.  It seems to be benevolent and restorative.
speaker: narrator

text: The rock faces are slippery with melted snow, and there is no need to climb the little tree.
speaker: narrator

text: The atmosphere here is peaceful and calm.   There is no need to throw anything.
speaker: narrator

text: You sleep comfortably among the fragrant flowers.
speaker: narrator

text: The small tree is most amazing.  It bears blossoms and fruit at the same time, and the fruit on its boughs seems to shyly appear and disappear, shimmering.
speaker: narrator

text: Never met her.  I only built this vacation home here a century ago.
speaker: erasmus

text: Erana has a nice little magical meadow in her name northeast of here.  Pretty respectable Spellcaster, I imagine.
speaker: erasmus

text: The Protection spell she cast over the town is fairly good, but I believe she missed a few places.
speaker: erasmus

text: You're just jealous because the only thing anyone named after you was a soft drink.
speaker: fenrus

text: I thought that "Erasmus' Rootin' Tootin' Root Beer" was a WONDERFUL name for a product!  It's a shame the company went broke after that.
speaker: erasmus

text: Erana's spell prevents the use of excessive violence or hostile magic within Spielburg's walls, with the exception of a couple of places.
speaker: erasmus

text: It looks to me from up here as if she missed the back part of the alley, and the spell applies only to areas in town above the ground.
speaker: erasmus

text: Pretty shoddy work, if you ask me!
